title: Women and Economics
author: Charlotte Perkins Gilman
description: In "Women and Economics," Charlotte Perkins Gilman presents a groundbreaking analysis of the socio-economic forces shaping women's roles in society at the turn of the 20th century. Through a blend of sociological insight and feminist critique, Gilman argues for women's economic independence and autonomy as essential to social progress. Drawing on historical context and contemporary issues, her writing style is both provocative and persuasive, inviting readers to rethink traditional gender roles
